description: 6–8 layers, all 3 risk categories present (High/Medium/Low), all scored, mix of controlWeakness values ('Weak controls', 'Strong controls', 'Low-risk area'). Tests standard 4-bullet path, correct bolding of layer names and weak signals.

6–8 layers, all 3 risk categories present (High/Medium/Low), all scored, mix of controlWeakness values ('Weak controls', 'Strong controls', 'Low-risk area'). Tests standard 4-bullet path, correct bolding of layer names and weak signals.

10+ layers, majority High Risk (>60%), several layers with 'Weak controls' and 'Control failure / anomaly'. Tests top-3 cap in priorityLayers, bold formatting for multiple negative signals, processCount reporting.

All scored layers in Low Risk category, all 'Low-risk area' controlWeakness, all Yearly frequency. Tests zero-suppression for High/Medium categories and correct Yearly assignment with no urgency bolding.
8 layers, only 2–3 scored, 5–6 unscored. Tests unscored count prominence in P1, and graceful handling when priorityLayers has only 2 items.

Multiple layers with 'Control failure / anomaly' (Inherent Low + Residual High). Also includes some 'Weak controls'. Tests bold formatting for anomaly signal and accurate narration of control weakness type in P2 and P4.
Only High Risk and Medium Risk present — no Low Risk layers at all. Tests zero-suppression for Low Risk category and correct Yearly bracket omission in P3.
All layers in a single risk category (e.g. all Medium Risk / Quarterly). Tests single-category narrative, 100% percent, and P3 with only one frequency bracket.
Only 1 layer in the entity (scored). Tests singular noun forms, 100% distribution, and priorityLayers with 1 item.
Multiple High Risk layers all with 'Strong controls' (Inherent High + Residual Low). Tests that 'Strong controls' is NOT bolded (not a negative signal) while layer names are still bolded in P2.
All layers unscored — riskDistribution.categories is empty, priorityLayers.items is empty. Tests graceful section collapse with 'Data not available for this section.' for P1/P2/P3/P4.
